title: provider_apply-patch-metadata-strip — C4 model
description: Component view: two surgical patch sites — apply_patch tool return value + UI consumer rewiring. No new components; no API surface change.

systems.name: OpenCode runtime
systems.description: Single-process bun daemon hosting session runloop, tools, message persistence, and web UI.

containers.name: Tool runtime
containers.description: packages/opencode/src/tool/* — owns tool definitions including apply_patch. Returns ToolResult to session processor for persistence.

containers.name: Session runtime
containers.description: packages/opencode/src/session/* — persists tool parts to parts.payload_json via Session.updatePart; toModelMessages serializes to LLM prompt.

containers.name: Web UI
containers.description: packages/ui/* — Solid.js components rendering session messages including apply_patch diffs.

containers.name: Snapshot store
containers.description: packages/opencode/src/snapshot/* — git-backed file-content history; canonical source for before/after reconstruction if ever needed.

components.name: apply_patch tool
components.description: Tool.define('apply_patch'). Patched: return value's metadata.files[] entries omit before/after.

components.name: toModelMessages
components.description: Serializes MessageV2 parts to ModelMessage[] for streamText. Reads state.output + state.attachments only; never reads state.metadata. UNCHANGED but quoted as invariant.

components.name: pruneToolMetadata
components.description: Strips oversized before/after from tool metadata during dream-pruning sweeps. UNCHANGED — retained for legacy session support.

components.name: message-part component
components.description: Renders message parts including apply_patch diffs. Patched: 3 sites switch from before/after feed to hunk-format diff feed; dead edit-tool references removed.

relationships.description: Tool result → persisted part → serialized to LLM (state.output only)
Tool result → persisted part → rendered in UI (uses metadata.files[].diff, NOT before/after after this change)
Calls Snapshot.track() to commit file content into git store (unchanged — canonical history source)
Operates on persisted parts post-hoc; near-no-op for new sessions, full effect for legacy
